raw_output_payload.reasoning:
Need bash command: find /testbed -name '*.php' -type f -print0 | xargs -0 cat | wc -l? But need per file counts and total. Use awk? Maybe: find ... -exec wc -l {} + | awk 'END{print "Total:", $1}'

But wc -l outputs lines count and filename. Sum. Use awk to sum first column. Command: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | xargs -0 wc -l | awk 'NR>1{sum+=$1} END{print sum}'

But also print per file? It says counts lines in each of *.php files and prints total count. So output per file counts and total. So: find ... -exec wc -l {} + | awk 'END{print "Total:", $1}'

But wc -l + prints each file and total. Actually wc -l with multiple files prints each and total. So just: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -exec wc -l {} +. That prints per file and total. So final command.
